entries.definition:
كتاب اللاّم

لغب

(لقي) اللام والقاف والحرف المعتل أصولٌ ثلاثة: أحدها يدلُّ على عوَج، والآخر على توافِي شيئين، والآخر على طَرْحِ شيء.

فالأوَّل اللَّقْوة: داءٌ يأخذ في الوجه يعوَجُّ منه. ورجل مَلْقُوٌّ، ولُقِيَ الإنسانُ. واللَّقْوة: الدَّلو التي إذا أرسلتَها في البِئر وارتفعت أخرى شالت معها . قال:

* شرُّ الدِّلاء اللَّقوة المُلازمهْ *

l–q–y to meet, to encounter, reunion; to find; to undergo, to
847
entries.definition_english:
l–q–y to meet, to encounter, reunion; to find; to undergo, to
847

entries.definition:
suffer, to experience; to throw, to cast; to give, to receive, to
accept; to lie down. Of this root, 16 forms occur 146 times in the
Qur’an: لَقِيَ laqiya 14 times; لَقَّى laqqā once; تُلَقَّى tulaqqā five
times; يُلَاقِي yulāqī three times; أَلْقَى ʾalqā 58 times; أُلْقِيَ ʾulqiya 13
times; تَلَقَّى talaqqā four times; الْتَقَى ʾiltaqā seven times; لَاقٍ lāqin
once; لِقَاء liqāʾ 24 times; تِلْقَاء tilqāʾ three times; تَلَاقٍ talāqin once;
مُلَاقٍ mulāqin seven times; مُلْقُون mulqūn three times; مُلْقِيَات
mulqiyāt once and مُتَلَقِّيَان mutalaqqiyān once.

لَقِيَ laqiya a [v. trans.] 1 to meet (2:14) وَإِذَا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَالُوا آمَنَّا
and when they meet the believers, they say, ‘We believe’ 2 to meet
in battle, to battle against (8:45) إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً when you meet a
force in battle, stand firm 3 to find (17:13) وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كِتَابًا
يَلْقَاهُ and We shall bring out for him, on the Day of Resurrection,
a record, which he will find spread wide open 4 to reap, to meet
with, to come face to face with, to reap the result of one’s deed
(19:59) فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا so they will meet with evil
[for what they have done] 5 to experience (18:62) لَقَدْ لَقِينَا مِنْ سَفَرِنَا
هَٰذَا نَصَبًا we have experienced, in this journeying of ours, fatigue.

لَقَّى laqqā [v. II, doubly trans.] to reward, to give (76:11) فَوَقَاهُمُ
اللَّهُ شَرَّ ذَٰلِكَ الْيَوْمِ وَلَقَّاهُمْ نَضْرَةً وَسُرُورًا so God protected them from the
evil of that Day, and rewarded them with radiance and gladness.

تُلَقَّى tulaqqā [pass. imperf. of v. لَقَّى laqqā] 1 to be made to
receive, to be given (27:6) وَإِنَّكَ لَتُلَقَّى الْقُرْآنَ مِن لَّدُنْ حَكِيمٍ عَلِيمٍ and you
[Prophet] are given the Qur’an from One who is All-Wise,
All-Knowing 2 to be granted (41:35) وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَمَا
يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ but none will be granted it save for the steadfast;
none will be granted it save for those endowed with great good
fortune.

يُلَاقِي yulāqī [imperf. of v. III, trans.] to meet with, to
encounter, to come face to face with (70:42) حَتَّىٰ يُلَاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي
يُوعَدُونَ so leave them to wallow in idle talk and jest,
until they come face to face with their promised Day.

أَلْقَى ʾalqā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to cast down, to throw down (26:32)

848
entries.definition_english:
suffer, to experience; to throw, to cast; to give, to receive, to
accept; to lie down. Of this root, 16 forms occur 146 times in the
Qur’an: لَقِيَ laqiya 14 times; لَقَّى laqqā once; تُلَقَّى tulaqqā five
times; يُلَاقِي yulāqī three times; أَلْقَى ʾalqā 58 times; أُلْقِيَ ʾulqiya 13
times; تَلَقَّى talaqqā four times; الْتَقَى ʾiltaqā seven times; لَاقٍ lāqin
once; لِقَاء liqāʾ 24 times; تِلْقَاء tilqāʾ three times; تَلَاقٍ talāqin once;

لَقِيَ laqiya a [v. trans.] 1 to meet (2:14) وَإِذَا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَالُوا آمَنَّا
and when they meet the believers, they say, ‘We believe’ 2 to meet
in battle, to battle against (8:45) إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً when you meet a
force in battle, stand firm 3 to find (17:13) وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كِتَابًا
يَلْقَاهُ and We shall bring out for him, on the Day of Resurrection,
a record, which he will find spread wide open 4 to reap, to meet
with, to come face to face with, to reap the result of one’s deed
(19:59) فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا so they will meet with evil
[for what they have done] 5 to experience (18:62) لَقَدْ لَقِينَا مِنْ سَفَرِنَا
هَٰذَا نَصَبًا we have experienced, in this journeying of ours, fatigue.

لَقَّى laqqā [v. II, doubly trans.] to reward, to give (76:11) فَوَقَاهُمُ
اللَّهُ شَرَّ ذَٰلِكَ الْيَوْمِ وَلَقَّاهُمْ نَضْرَةً وَسُرُورًا so God protected them from the
evil of that Day, and rewarded them with radiance and gladness.

تُلَقَّى tulaqqā [pass. imperf. of v. لَقَّى laqqā] 1 to be made to
receive, to be given (27:6) وَإِنَّكَ لَتُلَقَّى الْقُرْآنَ مِن لَّدُنْ حَكِيمٍ عَلِيمٍ and you
[Prophet] are given the Qur’an from One who is All-Wise,
All-Knowing 2 to be granted (41:35) وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَمَا
يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ but none will be granted it save for the steadfast;
none will be granted it save for those endowed with great good
fortune.

يُلَاقِي yulāqī [imperf. of v. III, trans.] to meet with, to
encounter, to come face to face with (70:42) حَتَّىٰ يُلَاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي
يُوعَدُونَ so leave them to wallow in idle talk and jest,
until they come face to face with their promised Day.

أَلْقَى ʾalqā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to cast down, to throw down (26:32)

848

entries.definition:
فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ so he cast down his staff and—lo and
behold!—it became a snake for everyone to see; * (50:37) أَلْقَى
السَّمْعَ listened in, lent an ear to [lit. cast the hearing] 2 to lay down
(16:15) وَأَلْقَىٰ فِي الْأَرْضِ رَوَاسِيَ and [He] set firm mountains
on the earth lest it sway under you 3 to bestow upon, to shower
upon (20:39) وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِّنِّي and I bestowed upon you love
from Me 4 to offer (4:90) وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ so if
they withdraw and do not fight you, and offer you peace; *(75:15)
أَلْقَىٰ مَعَاذِيرَهُ toss out excuses 5 to lay on, to place on (12:93) فَأَلْقُوهُ
عَلَىٰ وَجْهِ أَبِي يَأْتِ بَصِيرًا take this shirt of mine and lay
it over the face of my father—he will become sighted 6 to hurl into,
to cast into (50:24) أَلْقِيَا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ hurl every obstinate
disbeliever into Hell 7 to instigate, to foment, to sow (5:64) وَأَلْقَيْنَا
بَيْنَهُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ and We sowed enmity and hatred
amongst them till the Day of Resurrection 8 to bring forth, to cast
out (84:3–4) وَإِذَا الْأَرْضُ مُدَّتْ وَأَلْقَتْ مَا فِيهَا وَتَخَلَّتْ when the earth is
stretched out, and casts forth what is in it and empties itself 9 to
charge with, to entrust with (73:5) إِنَّا سَنُلْقِي عَلَيْكَ قَوْلًا ثَقِيلًا We shall
entrust you with a weighty discourse 10 to convey, to
communicate (4:171) وَكَلِمَتُهُ أَلْقَاهَا إِلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ the Messiah, Jesus, son of
Mary, was only a messenger of God, and His word, [that] He
conveyed to Mary 11 to retort (16:86) فَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلَ إِنَّكُمْ
لَكَاذِبُونَ when the idolaters see their partners [whom they ascribed
to God] they will say, ‘Our Lord, these are the partners we used
to invoke besides You,’ but the partners will retort, ‘Indeed, you
are liars.’

أُلْقِيَ ʾulqiya [pass., v. IV.] 1 to be hurled, to be cast (41:40) أَفَمَن
يُلْقَىٰ فِي النَّارِ خَيْرٌ is he who is hurled into the Fire better? 2 to be
dropped, to be thrown over, to be delivered (27:29) إِنِّي أُلْقِيَ إِلَيَّ كِتَابٌ
كَرِيمٌ a precious letter has been handed down to me 3 to be flung
down, to be reduced to falling down (26:46) فَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ سَاجِدِينَ and
the sorcerers were flung prostrate 4 to be revealed, to be sent
down (28:86) وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُو أَنْ يُلْقَىٰ إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ you
yourself could not have expected that the Book be revealed to
you—it came only as a mercy from your Lord.

849
entries.definition_english:
فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ so he cast down his staff and—lo and
behold!—it became a snake for everyone to see; * (50:37) أَلْقَى
السَّمْعَ listened in, lent an ear to [lit. cast the hearing] 2 to lay down
(16:15) وَأَلْقَىٰ فِي الْأَرْضِ رَوَاسِيَ and [He] set firm mountains
on the earth lest it sway under you 3 to bestow upon, to shower

they withdraw and do not fight you, and offer you peace; *(75:15)
أَلْقَىٰ مَعَاذِيرَهُ toss out excuses 5 to lay on, to place on (12:93) فَأَلْقُوهُ
عَلَىٰ وَجْهِ أَبِي يَأْتِ بَصِيرًا take this shirt of mine and lay
it over the face of my father—he will become sighted 6 to hurl into,
to cast into (50:24) أَلْقِيَا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ hurl every obstinate

amongst them till the Day of Resurrection 8 to bring forth, to cast
out (84:3–4) وَإِذَا الْأَرْضُ مُدَّتْ وَأَلْقَتْ مَا فِيهَا وَتَخَلَّتْ when the earth is
stretched out, and casts forth what is in it and empties itself 9 to
charge with, to entrust with (73:5) إِنَّا سَنُلْقِي عَلَيْكَ قَوْلًا ثَقِيلًا We shall
entrust you with a weighty discourse 10 to convey, to
communicate (4:171) وَكَلِمَتُهُ أَلْقَاهَا إِلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ the Messiah, Jesus, son of
Mary, was only a messenger of God, and His word, [that] He
conveyed to Mary 11 to retort (16:86) فَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلَ إِنَّكُمْ
لَكَاذِبُونَ when the idolaters see their partners [whom they ascribed
to God] they will say, ‘Our Lord, these are the partners we used
to invoke besides You,’ but the partners will retort, ‘Indeed, you
are liars.’

أُلْقِيَ ʾulqiya [pass., v. IV.] 1 to be hurled, to be cast (41:40) أَفَمَن
يُلْقَىٰ فِي النَّارِ خَيْرٌ is he who is hurled into the Fire better? 2 to be
dropped, to be thrown over, to be delivered (27:29) إِنِّي أُلْقِيَ إِلَيَّ كِتَابٌ
كَرِيمٌ a precious letter has been handed down to me 3 to be flung
down, to be reduced to falling down (26:46) فَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ سَاجِدِينَ and
the sorcerers were flung prostrate 4 to be revealed, to be sent
down (28:86) وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُو أَنْ يُلْقَىٰ إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ you
yourself could not have expected that the Book be revealed to
you—it came only as a mercy from your Lord.

849

entries.definition:
تَلَقَّى talaqqā [v. V, trans.] 1 to receive (2:37) فَتَلَقَّىٰ آدَمُ مِن رَّبِّهِ كَلِمَاتٍ
then Adam received words from his Lord 2 to meet, to
welcome (21:103) وَتَتَلَقَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ هَٰذَا يَوْمُكُمُ الَّذِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ the angels
will welcome them [saying,] ‘This is your Day, the one you were
promised!’

الْتَقَى ʾiltaqā [v. VIII, intrans.] to come face to face with one
another, to meet one another (8:44) وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ الْتَقَيْتُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِكُمْ
قَلِيلًا and [remember] when He showed them to you, when you came
face to face, as few in your eyes.

لَاقٍ lāqin [act. part.] one who finds something, one who meets
with someone (28:61) أَفَهُوَ لَاقِيهِ كَمَن مَّتَّعْنَاهُ مَتَاعَ الْحَيَاةِ
الدُّنْيَا is the one to whom We have made a
good promise, then he finds it [coming true], like the one We have
given some worldly enjoyment but on the Day of Resurrection will
be one of those taken to task?

لِقَاء liqāʾ [v. n.] meeting, to meet (18:110) فَمَن كَانَ يَرْجُو لِقَاءَ رَبِّهِ
so whoever hopes of meeting his Lord.

تِلْقَاء tilqāʾ [v. n. used adverbially] towards (7:47) وَإِذَا صُرِفَتْ
أَبْصَارُهُمْ تِلْقَاء أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ and when their sights are directed towards
the people of the Fire; *(10:15) مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي of my own accord.

تَلَاقٍ talāqin [v. n./n.] encountering, encounter (40:15) لِيُنذِرَ يَوْمَ
التَّلَاقِ to warn of the Day of Encounter.

مُلَاقٍ mulāqin [act. part.] one who meets, meets with (2:46) الَّذِينَ
يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَاقُو رَبِّهِمْ those who believe [also interpreted as: who
expect] that they will meet their Lord.

مُلْقِي mulqī [act. part.; fem. pl. مُلْقِيَات mulqiyāt] 1 one who casts
or throws something down (7:115) قَالُوا يَامُوسَىٰ إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ
so they said, ‘Moses, either you throw first or we will be
the ones to throw’ 2 one who brings down, one who delivers
(77:5–6) فَالْمُلْقِيَاتِ ذِكْرًا عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا and those delivering a message
that excuses or warns!’

مُتَلَقِّي mutalaqqī [act. part.] one receiving, receiver, receptor,
recording angel (50:17) إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى الْمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٌ when
the two receivers receive [him], one seated on the left [and the

other] on the right.

لكن lākin [rectifying particle (حرف استدراك) occurring some 65 times
in the Qur’an] but, yet, nevertheless, rather (43:76) وَمَا ظَلَمْنَاهُمْ وَلَٰكِن
كَانُوا هُمُ الظَّالِمِينَ We never wronged them; rather, they were the ones
who were the wrongdoers.

لكنّ lākinna [rectifying particle (حرف استدراك), one of the sisters of
إنّ ʾinna (من أخوات إنّ), (q.v.), occurring some 65 times in the Qur’an:
The use of lākinna, rather than lākin, (q.v.) lends emphasis to the
context] but in fact, on the contrary (2:102) وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَلَٰكِنَّ
الشَّيَاطِينَ كَفَرُوا not that Solomon himself was a disbeliever; it is the
devils who, in fact, were disbelievers.

لم lam [negative particle (حرف نفي) occurring some 346 times in the
Qur’an. It precedes an imperfect, in the jussive (مجزوم); the time
reference of the whole is similar to that of the perfect] did not
(112:3) لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ He did not father nor was He fathered.
entries.definition_english:
تَلَقَّى talaqqā [v. V, trans.] 1 to receive (2:37) فَتَلَقَّىٰ آدَمُ مِن رَّبِّهِ كَلِمَاتٍ
then Adam received words from his Lord 2 to meet, to

الدُّنْيَا is the one to whom We have made a

لِقَاء liqāʾ [v. n.] meeting, to meet (18:110) فَمَن كَانَ يَرْجُو لِقَاءَ رَبِّهِ
so whoever hopes of meeting his Lord.

تِلْقَاء tilqāʾ [v. n. used adverbially] towards (7:47) وَإِذَا صُرِفَتْ
أَبْصَارُهُمْ تِلْقَاء أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ and when their sights are directed towards
the people of the Fire; *(10:15) مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي of my own accord.

تَلَاقٍ talāqin [v. n./n.] encountering, encounter (40:15) لِيُنذِرَ يَوْمَ
التَّلَاقِ to warn of the Day of Encounter.

مُلَاقٍ mulāqin [act. part.] one who meets, meets with (2:46) الَّذِينَ
يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَاقُو رَبِّهِمْ those who believe [also interpreted as: who
expect] that they will meet their Lord.

مُلْقِي mulqī [act. part.; fem. pl. مُلْقِيَات mulqiyāt] 1 one who casts
or throws something down (7:115) قَالُوا يَامُوسَىٰ إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ
so they said, ‘Moses, either you throw first or we will be
the ones to throw’ 2 one who brings down, one who delivers
(77:5–6) فَالْمُلْقِيَاتِ ذِكْرًا عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا and those delivering a message
that excuses or warns!’

مُتَلَقِّي mutalaqqī [act. part.] one receiving, receiver, receptor,
recording angel (50:17) إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى الْمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٌ when
the two receivers receive [him], one seated on the left [and the

other] on the right.

لكن lākin [rectifying particle (حرف استدراك) occurring some 65 times
in the Qur’an] but, yet, nevertheless, rather (43:76) وَمَا ظَلَمْنَاهُمْ وَلَٰكِن
كَانُوا هُمُ الظَّالِمِينَ We never wronged them; rather, they were the ones
who were the wrongdoers.

لكنّ lākinna [rectifying particle (حرف استدراك), one of the sisters of
إنّ ʾinna (من أخوات إنّ), (q.v.), occurring some 65 times in the Qur’an:
The use of lākinna, rather than lākin, (q.v.) lends emphasis to the
context] but in fact, on the contrary (2:102) وَمَا كَفَرَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَلَٰكِنَّ
الشَّيَاطِينَ كَفَرُوا not that Solomon himself was a disbeliever; it is the
devils who, in fact, were disbelievers.

لم lam [negative particle (حرف نفي) occurring some 346 times in the
Qur’an. It precedes an imperfect, in the jussive (مجزوم); the time
reference of the whole is similar to that of the perfect] did not
(112:3) لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ He did not father nor was He fathered.
